metric_tooltips.metric_1: The macro-anchor of the simulation. When this drops below 45, the world enters Building Tensions phase. Below 70, decay accelerates across all other metrics.
metric_tooltips.metric_2: Weighted average of demographic approval. Derived from Working Class, Institutional Elites, and Global Allies. Hard to move quickly.
metric_tooltips.metric_3: Consumer Price Index. Tracks inflation and cost of living. High CPI destroys Working Class approval. The 'Boiling Frog' metric.
metric_tooltips.metric_4: Faith in state institutions (judiciary, intelligence, legislature). Hard to rebuild once lost. Determines legislative success rates.
metric_tooltips.metric_5: The Dow Jones equivalent. Reactive to nearly everything. Institutional Elites are extremely sensitive to this. High volatility periods see rapid swings.
metric_tooltips.metric_6: 10-Year Treasury Bond Yields. High yields signal that international markets see the U.S. as a risky borrower. Hard to reduce once elevated.
metric_tooltips.metric_7: Unemployment Rate. A lagging indicator — reacts slowly to decisions but has compounding political effects on Working Class approval over time.
metric_tooltips.metric_8: Global oil and energy price benchmark. The lever that connects most geopolitical events to domestic economic pain. Affects CPI directly.
metric_tooltips.metric_9: Scarcity Index. The strategic bottleneck. Reflects critical resource availability. High scarcity forces alternative policy paths regardless of other metrics.

1.name: Incremental Growth
1.description: Peacetime. Decisions involve mundane policy and slow-burn development.

2.name: Building Tensions
2.description: Geopolitical friction accelerates. Decay rates double. Allies grow nervous.

3.name: Flashpoint
3.description: Binary triggers become active. Systemic collapse is possible each turn.

events.title: The Annual Budget Hearing
events.description: The Congressional Budget Office projects a modest 1.2% deficit overage. The OMB director wants direction before the floor vote.

options.text: Fiscal Hawk: Force spending cuts across discretionary programs

SUCCESS.description: Markets approve. Bond yields tighten. But the working class feels the pinch.

FAILURE.description: The coalition falls apart. The vote fails publicly. A perception of dysfunction spreads.

options.text: Stimulus Package: Approve emergency infrastructure spending

SUCCESS.description: The stimulus lands. Unemployment ticks down. CPI creeps up slightly.

FAILURE.description: Supply chain bottlenecks absorb the funds. Scarcity paradox: more money, less goods.

options.text: Defer & Investigate: Commission an independent audit

SUCCESS.description: The audit reveals inefficiencies you can now champion publicly. Institutional trust ticks up.

FAILURE.description: The audit leaks. It looks like you were hiding something. Trust collapses.

options.text: Signal to Markets: Informal guidance on rate expectations

SUCCESS.description: The Fed signals in kind. Bond market stabilizes with barely a ripple.

FAILURE.description: Traders interpret the signal as dovish. CPI expectations jump.

events.title: The Silicon Shield Drops
events.description: Beijing has announced an 'Environmental Audit' on all semiconductor exports. Supply chains are freezing. The White House Situation Room is lit up.

options.text: File WTO Complaint & Subsidize Domestic Chip Fabrication

SUCCESS.description: Markets stabilize. TSMC's Arizona plant gets fast-tracked. But the cost of everything digital just went up.

FAILURE.description: The WTO complaint is dismissed. Silicon Valley is in freefall. The working class can't afford a laptop.

options.text: Transactional Pivot: Offer Tariff Rollbacks on Agricultural Goods

SUCCESS.description: A deal is struck. You traded corn for silicon. Markets breathe. Allies raise an eyebrow.

FAILURE.description: They rejected the deal and leaked the transcripts. You look weak and desperate.

options.text: Rally Allies: Coordinate a G7 Counter-Response

SUCCESS.description: The G7 presents a unified front. China pauses the audit. Global Stability recovers slightly.

FAILURE.description: Germany and France break ranks. The united front collapses in a press conference. Embarrassing.

options.text: Activate Strategic Reserve: Release rare earth stockpiles to industry

SUCCESS.description: The reserve buys 18 months. Industry stabilizes. You bought time, not a solution.

FAILURE.description: The reserve is smaller than classified documents suggested. Industry panics when the numbers leak.

options.text: Escalate: Place targeted sanctions on Chinese semiconductor firms

SUCCESS.description: The sanctions bite. Huawei's supply chain staggers. But oil prices jump as Beijing retaliates in energy markets.

FAILURE.description: The sanctions are porous. Chinese firms reroute through Malaysia. You've created friction with allies and accomplished nothing.

events.title: The Strait Goes Dark
events.description: IRGC remnants have mined the Strait of Hormuz. 3 tankers are dead in the water. Oil futures hit $145 in after-hours trading. The 5th Fleet is requesting orders.

options.text: Full Military Escort: Deploy carrier group to clear shipping lanes

SUCCESS.description: The Navy clears the path in 48 hours. Markets recover. You look decisive. The IRGC retreats but isn't finished.

FAILURE.description: A destroyer is hit by a drone swarm. 12 sailors KIA. The world is watching. Markets vanish.

options.text: Call Beijing: Request China lean on the IRGC remnants

SUCCESS.description: An unlikely back-channel works. The mines are removed, but China now has leverage over the Middle East architecture.

FAILURE.description: China demands too high a price. You hang up. The world sees it. The Strait stays dark.

options.text: Emergency Price Controls: Cap domestic gas prices by executive order

SUCCESS.description: Panic at the pump is averted. The working class gets a temporary break. Institutionalists hate it.

FAILURE.description: Companies hoard supply before the cap kicks in. Shortages emerge within 72 hours. Worse than before.

options.text: Surgical Strike: Target IRGC mine-laying infrastructure

SUCCESS.description: Precision munitions destroy the mine-laying vessels. The Strait reopens by morning. Allies rally. Adversaries recalculate.

FAILURE.description: Collateral damage hits a civilian structure. The UN Security Council is activated. You've handed Iran a propaganda gift.

events.title: WTO Dispute: Steel Tariff Challenge
events.description: The European Commission has filed a formal WTO challenge against existing U.S. steel tariffs. Brussels wants a response before the committee vote.

options.text: Negotiate: Offer a bilateral steel quota arrangement

SUCCESS.description: A quiet deal is struck. Steel workers keep their jobs. European allies maintain the relationship.

FAILURE.description: European negotiators reject the quota as insufficient. The WTO process resumes.

options.text: Stand Firm: Defend the tariffs as a national security measure

SUCCESS.description: The national security justification holds at the WTO. Precedent is set. Allies are frustrated but respect the resolve.

FAILURE.description: The WTO panel rules against you. Retaliatory tariffs on bourbon and motorcycles are approved.

options.text: Concede: Accept the challenge and phase out the tariffs over 3 years

SUCCESS.description: The orderly phase-out is praised as pragmatic leadership. Transatlantic relations warm noticeably.

FAILURE.description: Steel union endorsements evaporate overnight. The working class sees this as capitulation.
